items.summary: Cover of Netzwerk neu A1 coursebook by Klett Sprachen.
Imprint page: authors, ISBN, and audio and video download codes.
Legend explaining the pictograms and icons used throughout the coursebook.
Table of contents for chapters 1 to 3 with goals and grammar.
Table of contents for chapters 4 to 6 with goals and grammar.
Table of contents for chapters 7 to 9 with goals and grammar.
Table of contents for chapters 10 to 12 plus appendix listing.
Chapter 1 opener: match international German loanwords to their meanings.
German loanword cards; translate them and collect more examples.
Informal greetings dialogues: Hallo and Tschüs, asking names and wellbeing.
Formal greetings: Guten Tag, introductions, and verb conjugation practice.
Asking origin, residence, and languages; W-questions and verb endings.
Numbers to 20, the alphabet, spelling phone numbers and emails.
Reading profiles; complete a table of people's countries and languages.
More profiles; match countries with languages, then speak and write.
Netzwerk-WG video tasks: who lives there, matching info and speech bubbles.
Chapter 1 review: phrases and grammar summary, kurz und klar.
Chapter 2 opener: listen and note what people like doing.
Tick which hobbies you like, then ask and answer what you enjoy doing.
Complete verbs and personal endings about Sophie's photos; conjugation practice game.
Making cinema dates: weekdays, question intonation, and appointment dialogues.
Match objects to four jobs, read job profiles, and practise big numbers.
Note plural forms and endings, match jobs to pictures, discuss work times.
Find gender and plural in dictionary entries; sort nouns and label an article picture.
Match personal-data labels, then fill in a sports-club registration form.
Video scenes 4-5: note hobbies and identify Luca and Anna's jobs.
Chapter 2 review: hobby and job phrases, numbers, verb and article grammar.
Chapter 3 opener: Hamburg tour, match stations to photos, fill in facts.
Hamburg harbour, Michel and town hall; gather facts about your own city.
Taxi ride to hotel: mark named places, read dialogue, fill in articles.
Definite and indefinite articles with Hamburg places; long-short vowel listening.
Transport picture story; match captions and practise negation with kein.
Asking and giving directions: listening tasks plus dialogue and dice game.
Read Hamburg event listings, match photos, and spot international cognate words.
Name months and seasons, match photos to seasons, note what people do when.
Munich video tour: number stations, match landmarks, fill facts, order directions.
Chapter 3 review (kurz und klar): phrases and grammar for places, directions, articles, imperative.
Revision board game (Wiederholungsspiel): mixed A1 game-field tasks plus an alphabet chart.
Revision board game continued: fill-in and speaking tasks across chapters 1 to 3.
Letter and vowel games: supply missing vowels, correct wrong vowels, number-dictation drawing.
Personen-Memo: read profiles of famous D-A-CH people and match those with the same job.
D-A-CH city quiz: name countries and capitals; fill gaps in Zürich, Wien and Graz texts.
More D-A-CH city texts (Leipzig, Köln, Genf): fill words, listen and locate three speakers.
Chapter 4 opener Guten Appetit!: breakfast and lunch food vocabulary; match words, listen for shops.
Food vocabulary (coffee and cake, dinner); tick the words you hear and sort foods into shops.
Planning a grill party: pick the right reply, write shopping lists, Akkusativ articles, umlaut sounds.
Supermarket shopping dialogues: match each to a picture and practise saying prices.
Grill-party mealtime dialogues: match to photos, practise möchten, talk about food likes.
Read three eating-habits texts, fill a meals-of-the-day table, then talk and write your own.
Vocabulary-learning strategies: build a food mindmap, learn word pairs and thematic word groups.
Read about a cook at Lake Constance and answer W-questions about his job.
Netzwerk-WG video: pick the shopping list, complete words, order the baking-afternoon photos.
Chapter 4 review (kurz und klar): shopping and eating phrases and grammar (articles, möchten, essen, mögen).
Chapter 5 opener Alltag und Familie: order Kaan's day photos, listen and number, mark Sunday activities.
Daily-routine photo collage: describe photos, tell about your day, present a five-photo day.
Telling the time: listen and match clocks, note official times, ask and answer.
Dobart family calendar: complete time phrases, true/false listening, possessive articles, r/a sound rule.
Dobart family homepage and guestbook: match captions to photos, complete possessive articles.
Making arrangements: read Mara's email, mark modal verbs, complete Johanna's modal-verb reply.
Making an appointment by phone: match dialogue lines, politeness box, role-play scheduling.
Punctuality: judge acceptable lateness, listen and note delays, match apology and reply phrases.
Netzwerk-WG video: order jogging-scene statements, fill in who does what, predict the outcome.
Chapter 5 review: phrases and grammar for time, appointments, apologies, possessives, modal verbs.
Chapter 6 opener Zeit mit Freunden: guess free-time activities from photos, compare loanwords.
Free time continued: collect words for photos, listen and name activities, mime and guess.
Dates: read the birthday-plan chat, complete sentences, listen for birthdays and diphthongs.
Birthday invitation: read the mail, practise separable verbs, interview a partner, write an invitation.
Restaurant: describe a dog scene, tick what Jan and Leela order, fill accusative pronouns.
Paying in a restaurant: menu role-play, order the paying dialogue, tipping and past-tense recap.
Read about D-A-CH cafes, beer gardens, beach bars and pubs; fill a venue table.
Read five event ads for missing details, listen to complete them, plan outings together.
Netzwerk-WG video: match party words and menu, judge statements, order Bea's phone-call dialogue.
Chapter 6 review: phrases for invitations, ordering food, events, dates and grammar.
Review board game: vocabulary, birthday and family-dialogue speaking prompts for two teams.
Review game continued: verb conjugation, naming words with articles, sentence-building prompts.
Time theme: speak about activities, arrange dates, dictate daily-routine poems in pairs.
Sort nine photos into much-time or little-time; discuss your own time use.
Eight regional D-A-CH dishes on a map; say which you know and would try.
Read four people's texts about favourite dishes and match each text to a dish.
Chapter 7 opener Arbeitsalltag: describe Adnan's morning; order two workplace dialogues.
Order the second workplace dialogue, role-play it, then describe your own morning.
Read Laura's internship blog; find likes and dislikes; join sentences with und/oder/aber.
Listen to Laura's workplace questions; Dativ practice; s/sch and st/scht pronunciation.
Listen, mark true/false, then match location sentences (Wohin/Wo/Woher) to pictures.
Build word-order sentences about your day; match office computer actions to pictures.
Read a formal club invitation letter; label letter parts; write a short reply.
Read about good and bad small-talk topics; match statements to conversation topics.
Netzwerk-WG video: order Anna's errands; fill printing-dialogue gaps; mark correct facts.
Chapter 7 review: sequencing, letter standards, small talk, Dativ and connectors grammar.
Chapter 8 opener Fit und gesund: describe photos, read Annika's health-experiment diary.
Continue Annika's health diary; listen to voice messages about her experiment and mark opinions.
Read the Fitness-App comic, mark imperatives, and play Fitness-App giving commands.
Fitness studio: match age/height/weight answers, label body parts, hear p/b, t/d, k/g.
Doctor visit after a bike accident: match sentences to photos and order the dialogue.
At the doctor: match complaints and advice to patients; write müssen/dürfen sentences.
Home remedies forum: read posts and match each user to the right tip.
Hospital professions: match jobs to photos and texts; learn word-decoding strategies.
Netzwerk-WG video: Luca's backache; assign lines, order the dialogue, note commands.
Chapter 8 review: personal info, doctor talk, imperative and modal verbs (dürfen/sollen) grammar.
Chapter 9 opener Meine Wohnung: furniture and appliance picture vocabulary; place items in rooms.
More furniture vocabulary; listen for four people's favourite spots at home.
Apartment search: read Carla and Alex's message, fill their wanted-ad, compare five listings.
New flat: sort furniture into rooms (wohin+Akkusativ); read and reply to a party invitation.
Housewarming picture: say where furniture is using wo+Dativ location prepositions.
Express liking/disliking a flat; long vs short e pronunciation; match furniture to colours.
Match five housing-type headlines to their photos (high-rise, loft, terraced, old building).
Note housing pros and cons from audio; read three residents' texts; write about your home.
Netzwerk-WG video: mark rooms Anna shows; describe furnishing changes; talk about guests.
Chapter 9 review: phrases for flats, invitations, likes/dislikes and two-way prepositions.
Revision board game part 1: speaking prompts on computers, health, home and invitations.
Revision board game part 2: prompts on furniture, colours, prepositions and pronunciation.
Fitness survey and partner interview about weekly exercise; write a can't-attend-class message.
Review: place furniture (polite/rude), describe cluttered-room pictures, play a word-staircase game.
Song 'Like, like' by Einshoch6: note words, guess the theme, order verses, discuss social media.
'Which living type are you?' quiz: seven multiple-choice questions plus a scoring key.
Chapter 10 opener 'Studium und Beruf': describe job photos; listen to a radio show on professions.
Rank what matters to you in a job (eight statements), then compare and make a class statistic.
Read Tina and Daniel's chat, order the day's pictures, complete Perfekt sentences (Satzklammer).
Listen and tick what Tina/Daniel did; match sentence halves; practise Partizip II (-ieren, irregular).
Read four people's job-search stories; check true/false; learn Perfekt with sein.
Interview classmates about the past; retell Florian's dramatic day; write about yesterday; h-sound drill.
Order a job-inquiry phone dialogue; learn telephone phrases; role-play a phone call.
Read about three D-A-CH festivals; note facts; hear which seasonal jobs the people do.
Netzwerk-WG video: where Bea and Anna work; read a job ad and Max's promoter message.
Chapter 10 review: phrases for jobs and telephoning, plus Perfekt grammar (haben/sein, Partizip II).
Chapter 11 opener 'Die Jacke gefällt mir!': match speech bubbles to a clothes-shopping comic; hear two dialogues.
Clothing picture story; collect clothing words on a mind map, say when you wear them.
Where to buy clothes; listen to Andreas shopping and learn welcher/dieser and clothing words.
Read chat reactions to new clothes; learn past-participle formation and write past-tense sentences.
Clothing-shop dialogues: match questions to answers and complete sentences with dative pronouns.
Department-store floor directory: listen for the Wagners' purchases and locate items by floor.
Read a Berlin fashion-scene infotext, pick a headline, and profiles of three shops.
Match Berlin shop tips to keyword categories; choose two places to research and present.
Video: watch Max choose an outfit; note his clothes and who attends a wedding.
Chapter 11 review: clothes-shopping phrases and grammar (welcher/dieser, dative verbs, participles).
Chapter 12 travel opener: match holiday types to photos and pack the right gear.
Listen for a travel destination and packing list; play the suitcase-packing memory game.
City-trip suggestions in Basel: tick heard activities, learn man, complete a tram route.
Describe a route, read a postcard, join sentences with denn, and write your own.
Read two travel reports, match headlines, answer comprehension questions, and complete question words.
Read Christian's ski-trip report, write questions, practise time prepositions and f/v/w pronunciation.
Place cities by compass direction, match holiday messages to weather reports, learn weather words.
Jigsaw reading on where Germans holiday (sea, cities, mountains); report and write your own.
Video: the flatmates' farm-holiday scenes; answer questions, order dialogues, describe Bea's Bremen trip.
Chapter 12 review of phrases for directions, postcards, weather, sequence and travel grammar.
Review speaking game: role-play a holiday day — pack a suitcase, buy a souvenir.
Holiday role-play continued: plan the day together and discuss it at dinner.
Word games: recount yesterday with sequence words, match questions to answers, timed vocabulary duel.
Read colleagues' emails to identify Andrea's delegated tasks and what remains for Monday.
D-A-CH country quiz part 1: German capital, population, landmarks, neighbours, regional foods, Swiss capital.
D-A-CH quiz part 2: Swiss languages/population, Austrian capital, flag, population; odd-one-out Vienna photo.
Grammar reference: statement sentences, word order, W-questions and yes/no questions.
Grammar reference: imperatives, connectors und/oder/aber/denn, present-tense conjugation and irregular verbs.
Grammar reference: sein/haben/werden, wissen, modal verbs, imperative, Präteritum and Perfekt.
Grammar reference: past participle formation and the sentence bracket (Satzklammer).
Grammar reference: nouns, definite/indefinite/negation articles, plurals and case forms.
Grammar reference: article cases, possessive, interrogative and demonstrative articles.
Grammar reference: adjectives, personal pronouns, man, and prepositions für/mit.
Grammar reference: time and date expressions, place prepositions and Wechselpräpositionen.
In-preposition tables, question words, and the start of the irregular-verb list.
Irregular verb list continued, plus special verbs and modal verbs reference.
Alphabetical word list: how to read entries, plus first A-entries.
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters A to B (Anweisung–Bett).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters B to E (bewegen–Ehefrau).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters E to F (Ehemann–frei).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters F to H (frei–Hand).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters H to K (Händler–Keks).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters K to M (Kellner–Mann).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters M to N (Mann–November).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters N to R (Nudel–reisen).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters R to S (Reisetasche–Ski-Urlaub).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters S to T (Small Talk–Toilette).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters T to V (toll–Vorschlag).
Alphabetical word list appendix, letters V to Z (vorspielen–zwölf).
